steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage concludes that Stobbe had better-explained methodology than Streicher-Porte. The passage defines "better explained methodology" through traceability scores — how clearly procedures are described. To weaken the conclusion, a choice must show Stobbe's traceability was not higher.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The study by Stobbe had a lower traceability than the study by Streicher-Porte and colleagues did.’: directly contradicts the conclusion — if Stobbe scored lower on the metric that defines "better explained," the conclusion fails. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘The study by Stobbe had a high consistency rating and a high traceability rating.’: supports the conclusion rather than weakening it. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The study by Stobbe had a lower consistency rating than the study by Streicher-Porte and colleagues did.’: neutral at best; doesn't clearly undermine the claim. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The study by Streicher-Porte and colleagues had a medium consistency rating and a medium traceability rating.’: measures something other than the passage's stated metric. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
